div.head: The Ambassador in Argentina (Weddell) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 172. Because of a recent slight indisposition of the Minister for Foreign Affairs I was only able yesterday to discuss with him the Department’s circular telegram of September 23, 2 p.m., when I emphasized to him that its subject matter was brought to his attention as a matter of interest to all members of the Pan American Union and not as a request for action.

The Minister for Foreign Affairs assured me of his interest but pointed out that with the adjournment of the League Assembly it would appear that nothing could be done at the moment; however, in my presence he directed that instructions be sent to his delegate in Geneva to get into contact with our Consul there with a view to keeping in touch with the subject and for appropriate action at the first available opportunity.
